expectations.text: Subject line is 2-4 words, lowercase, looks like internal communication
expectations.evidence: Subject: '**Subject:** Your routes are costing you 18% more than they should' (11 words, lowercase=False)

expectations.text: Email is ultra-brief (under 80 words for C-suite)
Opening is direct and peer-level (no deferential language)
Value is communicated in terms David cares about (cost savings, delivery performance, competitive advantage)
One proof point that speaks to business outcomes (fuel costs, on-time delivery rate)
CTA is extremely low-friction for a CEO (one-line reply, not a meeting request)
Sounds like a peer who understands logistics business, not a tech vendor
